name: circleci.com
prose:
# CircleCI REST API API

The CircleCI API is a RESTful, fully-featured API that allows you to do almost anything in CircleCI. You can access all information and trigger all actions. The only thing we don’t provide access to is billing functions, which must be done from the CircleCI web UI.

## Getting started

The CircleCI REST API API is a JSON REST API. All endpoints are relative to the base URL `https://circleci.com/api/v1`.

## Authentication

Authenticate by passing your API key in the `circle-token` header on every request.

## API reference

### GET /me
Provides information about the signed in user.
Returns a single record.

### GET /project/{username}/{project}
Build summary for each of the last 30 builds for a single git repo.
Returns a list of records.
Accepts query parameters: `limit`, `offset`, `filter`.

Sample response:

```json
[ { "id": 1 }, { "id": 2 } ]
```

### POST /project/{username}/{project}
Triggers a new build, returns a summary of the build.

### DELETE /project/{username}/{project}/build-cache
Clears the cache for a project.

### GET /project/{username}/{project}/checkout-key
Lists checkout keys.
Returns a list of records.

Sample response:

```json
[ { "id": 1 }, { "id": 2 } ]
```

### POST /project/{username}/{project}/checkout-key
Creates a new checkout key. Only usable with a user API token.

### GET /project/{username}/{project}/checkout-key/{fingerprint}
Get a checkout key.
Returns a single record.

### DELETE /project/{username}/{project}/checkout-key/{fingerprint}
Delete a checkout key.

### GET /project/{username}/{project}/envvar
Lists the environment variables for :project
Returns a single record.

### POST /project/{username}/{project}/envvar
Creates a new environment variable

### GET /project/{username}/{project}/envvar/{name}
Gets the hidden value of environment variable :name
Returns a single record.

### DELETE /project/{username}/{project}/envvar/{name}
Deletes the environment variable named ':name'

### POST /project/{username}/{project}/ssh-key
Create an ssh key used to access external systems that require SSH key-based authentication

### POST /project/{username}/{project}/tree/{branch}
Triggers a new build, returns a summary of the build. Optional build parameters can be set using an experimental API. Note: For more about build parameters, read about [using param

### GET /project/{username}/{project}/{build_num}
Full details for a single build. The response includes all of the fields from the build summary. This is also the payload for the [notification webhooks](/docs/configuration/#notif
Returns a single record.

### GET /project/{username}/{project}/{build_num}/artifacts
List the artifacts produced by a given build.
Returns a list of records.

Sample response:

```json
[ { "id": 1 }, { "id": 2 } ]
```

### POST /project/{username}/{project}/{build_num}/cancel
Cancels the build, returns a summary of the build.

### POST /project/{username}/{project}/{build_num}/retry
Retries the build, returns a summary of the new build.

### GET /project/{username}/{project}/{build_num}/tests
Provides test metadata for a build Note: [Learn how to set up your builds to collect test metadata](https://circleci.com/docs/test-metadata/)
Returns a single record.

### GET /projects
List of all the projects you're following on CircleCI, with build information organized by branch.
Returns a list of records.

Sample response:

```json
[ { "id": 1 }, { "id": 2 } ]
```

### GET /recent-builds
Build summary for each of the last 30 recent builds, ordered by build_num.
Returns a list of records.
Accepts query parameters: `limit`, `offset`.

Sample response:

```json
[ { "id": 1 }, { "id": 2 } ]
```

### POST /user/heroku-key
Adds your Heroku API key to CircleCI, takes apikey as form param name.
